title: Design conditional writes with optimistic locking (receiveIf)

pr.title: feat(core): conditional writes with optimistic locking (receiveIf)

markdown:
## Goal

Add version tracking to persistence records and implement `receiveIf()` -- a conditional write primitive that enables optimistic locking. This prevents lost-update problems where two clients read the same record, both modify it, and the second write silently overwrites the first.

## Background

Today, `PersistenceRecord` has only `{ ts, data }`. There is no mechanism for a client to say "write this only if the record hasn't changed since I last read it." Every `receive()` call is an unconditional overwrite.

The simplest approach: add a `version` field to `PersistenceRecord` (monotonically incrementing integer, starting at 1). The `receiveIf` method accepts an `expectedVersion` option. If the current version does not match, the write is rejected with a clear error.

Content-hash-as-version was considered but rejected: it conflates "data changed" with "version advanced," making idempotent rewrites fail unexpectedly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `PersistenceRecord` has an optional `version?: number` field
- [ ] `ReceiveResult` has an optional `version?: number` field (returns new version on success)
- [ ] A new `ConditionalWriteOptions` type exists: `{ expectedVersion?: number }`
- [ ] `NodeProtocolWriteInterface` gains `receiveIf<D>(msg: Message<D>, options: ConditionalWriteOptions): Promise<ReceiveResult>`
- [ ] `MemoryClient` implements `receiveIf` with version tracking (version starts at 1, increments on each write)
- [ ] `MemoryClient.read()` returns `version` in the record
- [ ] `receiveIf` with wrong `expectedVersion` returns `{ accepted: false, error: "version_conflict", version: currentVersion }`
- [ ] `receiveIf` without `expectedVersion` behaves like unconditional `receive()` (backwards compatible)
- [ ] `createValidatedClient` passes through `receiveIf` to the write backend
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass (version field is optional, non-breaking)
- [ ] New tests cover: first write (version=1), conditional update (version=2), conflict rejection, missing expectedVersion

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` to understand current `PersistenceRecord`, `ReceiveResult`, `NodeProtocolWriteInterface`
2. Add `version?: number` to `PersistenceRecord`
3. Add `version?: number` to `ReceiveResult`
4. Create `ConditionalWriteOptions` type: `{ expectedVersion?: number }`
5. Add `receiveIf<D>(msg: Message<D>, options: ConditionalWriteOptions): Promise<ReceiveResult>` to `NodeProtocolWriteInterface`
6. Read `libs/b3nd-client-memory/mod.ts` -- implement `receiveIf`:
   - Track version per URI in a separate `Map<string, number>`
   - On write: if `expectedVersion` is provided and doesn't match current version, reject
   - On successful write: increment version, include in result
   - On `read()`: include version in returned record
7. Read `libs/b3nd-compose/validated-client.ts` -- make `createValidatedClient` pass `receiveIf` through to the write backend (validate first, then call `write.receiveIf`)
8. Add tests in `libs/b3nd-client-memory/mod_test.ts`:
   - First write returns version=1
   - Read returns version=1
   - receiveIf with expectedVersion=1 succeeds, returns version=2
   - receiveIf with expectedVersion=1 (stale) fails with version_conflict
   - receiveIf without expectedVersion succeeds (unconditional)

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` -- add version to PersistenceRecord, ReceiveResult, add ConditionalWriteOptions, add receiveIf to interface
- `libs/b3nd-client-memory/mod.ts` -- implement receiveIf with version tracking
- `libs/b3nd-compose/validated-client.ts` -- wire receiveIf through validation
- `libs/b3nd-client-memory/mod_test.ts` -- new tests for version tracking and conditional writes

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-client-memory/
```

Verify all existing tests still pass:
```bash
deno test --allow-all --ignore=libs/b3nd-client-postgres,libs/b3nd-client-mongo libs/b3nd-*/
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: Nothing -- this is a foundational change
- **Blocks**: G1-A4-02 (all other clients implementing receiveIf)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design `receiveIf(uri, data, { expectedVersion })` — conditional writes with optimistic locking
- [ ] Add version tracking to records (or use content hash as version)
- [ ] Update all client implementations to support conditional writes
- [ ] Document concurrency model and recommended patterns
